paper.title: The fastest stars in the Galaxy

extraction.summary: This paper searches for hypervelocity white dwarfs (D^6 stars) ejected from thermonuclear supernovae using Gaia DR3 astrometry and spectroscopic follow-up. ADQL queries selected 25 blue candidates with high tangential velocities; spectroscopy confirmed 6 new discoveries (4 hot D^6 + 2 LP 40-365), plus 3 known D^6 stars for a total of 16 thermonuclear runaway candidates. The paper explicitly states that D^6 star velocities significantly exceed the Milky Way's escape velocity ('These velocities significantly exceed the Milky Way's escape velocity'), and that 'all of the D^6 stars will leave the Milky Way'. A total of 7 D^6 stars are confirmed as Galactic-unbound HVS candidates: D6-1, D6-2, D6-3 (Shen 2018), J1235-3752, J0927-6335, J0546+0836, J1332-3541 (newly discovered). J0927 and J1235 have the fastest known stellar radial velocities (-2285 and -1694 km/s).

method_chain.summary: Gaia DR3 ADQL query: selected candidates from gaiadr3.gaia_source. Conditions: G<20 mag, ruwe<1.4, pm>50 mas/yr, 4.74*pm/(parallax+parallax_error)>400 km/s, 4.74*pm/parallax>600 km/s, bp_rp<0.5 mag, parallax_over_error<5. Returned 25 candidates, excluded 2 sources with bright neighbors, 23 as highest priority. Also selected 21 sources with lower proper motion / higher parallax significance as a comparison sample.

source_refs.context: ADQL query implementation and selection criteria

method_chain.summary: Spectroscopic follow-up: used MagE (Magellan 6.5m, R~5500), LRIS (Keck-I 10m, R~1000-1500), ESI (Keck-II 10m, R~8000), SOAR/Goodman (4.1m), DBSP (Palomar 5m), GMOS (Gemini-South 8.1m), Kast (Lick 3m) for spectroscopic observations. Prioritized observing the brightest and highest tangential velocity candidates. RV measured via cross-correlation.
inputs: MagE, LRIS, ESI, SOAR, DBSP, GMOS, Kast spectra

source_refs.context: Spectroscopic follow-up summary: 39 candidates observed

method_chain.summary: Radial velocity measurement: RV measured by cross-correlation matching with synthetic spectra (ATLAS12/SYNTHE). Cross-correlation performed using MagE, LRIS, ESI, SOAR, DBSP, GMOS, Kast spectra.
inputs: Observed spectra (MagE, LRIS, ESI, SOAR, DBSP, GMOS, Kast)

source_refs.context: Spectral modeling with ATLAS12/SYNTHE

method_chain.summary: Spectral classification and stellar parameters: synthetic spectra computed using ATLAS12 (atmospheric structure) and SYNTHE (radiative transfer). C/O-dominated atmosphere (~70% C, 30% O, Mg/Si trace) and pure He atmosphere grids. Classification: 4 hot D6 (hydrogen-free, Teff=20000-130000K, |RV|>1000 km/s), 2 LP 40-365, the rest are DA WD/sdO/B/MS false positives. Teff and atmospheric composition estimated.

source_refs.context: Spectral modeling with ATLAS12/SYNTHE

method_chain.summary: Bayesian distance estimation: posterior sampling from (M_G,0, mu_alpha*, mu_delta, RV). M_G,0 flat prior (equivalent to p(d) proportional to 1/d). Parallax prediction: varpi=10^((M_G,0-G+10+A_G)/5). emcee sampling (64 walkers, 1000 burn-in + 1000 steps). Three distance priors tested (flat M_G,0 / exponentially decaying space density L=1.35 kpc / flat distance) as sensitivity analysis.

source_refs.context: Kinematic modeling: Bayesian inference with emcee
Three distance priors tested

method_chain.summary: 3D velocity and trajectory calculation: ignoring the Galactic gravitational potential (extremely high velocities, flight time ~1 Myr), straight-line trajectories. 3D velocity posterior derived from distance posterior and proper motions/RV. Total space velocity v_tot computed.

source_refs.context: Kinematic modeling: Bayesian inference with emcee

method_chain.summary: Ejection velocity calculation: v_ejection = |v - v_rot,birth|. v_rot,birth is the circular orbit velocity at the intersection of the trajectory with the Galactic disk (or at the current position), assuming a flat rotation curve v_circ=240 km/s. Prior: v_ejection < 3000 km/s. The paper states that D^6 star velocities significantly exceed the Milky Way's escape velocity, and all will leave the Milky Way for intergalactic space.

source_refs.context: Ejection velocity calculation and Galactic rotation subtraction
These velocities significantly exceed the Milky Way's escape velocity
In the future, all of the D^6 stars will leave the Milky Way
Gaia DR3 ADQL query used to select candidates

inclusion_assessment.summary: J0927-6335 is a newly discovered hot D^6 star in this paper, with the fastest stellar RV ever recorded (-2285+/-20 km/s). v_tot ~2753 km/s, v_ejection ~2519 km/s. The paper explicitly classifies it as a D^6 star and confirms its velocity far exceeds the Milky Way escape velocity. Carbon/oxygen-dominated atmosphere, Teff~60000K.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: The paper explicitly states that all D^6 stars exceed the Milky Way escape velocity and will leave the Galaxy. J0927 is one of the paper's core discoveries, with the fastest RV.

source_refs.context: Abstract: two stars have RVs of -1694 and -2285 km/s -- the fastest systemic stellar RVs ever measured
Velocities significantly exceed Milky Way's escape velocity, runaway WDs launched into intergalactic space
Table tab:query shows J0927-6335 as new hot D^6 star discovery

distance.description: Bayesian kinematic distance (flat M_G,0 prior). Flat distance prior variant: 5.40^+1.82_-1.99 kpc. EDSD prior variant: 4.35^+1.89_-1.41 kpc.

radial_velocity.description: Fastest systemic stellar RV ever measured. Measured from MagE spectrum cross-correlation.

total_velocity.description: Total 3D space velocity (heliocentric, straight-line trajectory, no Galactic potential).

galactic_rest_frame_velocity.description: Ejection velocity = |v - v_rot,birth|. Subtracts Galactic rotation (flat 240 km/s). Prior: v_ejection < 3000 km/s.

spectroscopy.description: Hot D^6 star: hydrogen-free, carbon/oxygen-dominated atmosphere, Teff~60000K. Classified as thermonuclear supernova runaway donor (D^6 scenario).

source_refs.context: Table tab:query: J0927 classified as hot D^6 star, MagE instrument

spectral_type.description: Carbon/oxygen atmosphere, Teff~60000K from Werner et al. (2024, A&A 682, A42) NLTE analysis.

source_refs.context: Three objects with Teff above 60000K, one above 100000K
Gaia DR3 ADQL query used to select candidates

inclusion_assessment.summary: J1235-3752 is a newly discovered hot D^6 star in this paper, with the second-fastest stellar RV (-1694+/-10 km/s). v_tot ~2670 km/s, v_ejection ~2471 km/s. Carbon-dominated atmosphere, Teff~20000-25000K, the coolest and most inflated among the hot D^6 stars. The paper clearly confirms its velocity far exceeds the Milky Way escape velocity.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: One of the paper's core discoveries, with the second-fastest known stellar RV. The paper explicitly states all D^6 stars will leave the Milky Way.

source_refs.context: Abstract: two stars have RVs of -1694 and -2285 km/s
J1235-3752: RV=-1694±10 km/s, C II, O II, Mg II lines

spectroscopy.description: Hot D^6 star: carbon-dominated atmosphere (C II, O II, Mg II, Si II/III). Teff 15000-25000K. Coolest of the new hot D^6 stars.

Gaia DR3 ADQL query used to select candidates

inclusion_assessment.summary: D6-1 is a known D^6 star discovered by Shen et al. (2018). RV=1200+/-40 km/s, v_tot~2045 km/s, v_ejection~2254 km/s. The paper confirms D^6 star velocities exceed the Milky Way escape velocity. Distance 1.91 kpc, one of the nearest D^6 stars.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Known D^6 star, included in the paper's census table with confirmation that all D^6 stars will leave the Milky Way. Its hypervelocity nature was already confirmed in Shen 2018.

source_refs.context: Table tab:all_systems: D6-1 listed as suspected D^6 star from Shen 2018
Shen 2018 discovered three D^6 stars including D6-1

citation_context_refs.context: Shen2018 discovered three D6 stars with large apparent tangential velocities and well-constrained parallaxes.

galactic_rest_frame_velocity.description: Ejection velocity relative to Galactic rotation.

spectroscopy.description: Known D^6 star: cool and puffy, Teff~7000K, R~0.2 R_sun, metal-line spectrum, no hydrogen.

source_refs.context: D6-1: Teff~7000K, R~0.2 R_sun, metal-line atmosphere

spectral_type.description: Cool and puffy D^6 star. Teff~7000K, R~0.2 R_sun.

source_refs.context: Three D^6 stars from Shen 2018: Teff 6000-7000K, radii 0.2-0.4 R_sun
Gaia DR3 ADQL query used to select candidates

inclusion_assessment.summary: D6-3 is a known D^6 star discovered by Shen et al. (2018). RV near 0 (-20+/-80 km/s) but v_tot~2248 km/s, v_ejection~2393 km/s, driven by extremely high tangential velocity. The paper confirms D^6 star velocities exceed the Milky Way escape velocity.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Known D^6 star. Despite low RV, its total space velocity and ejection velocity are extremely high (>2300 km/s), far exceeding the Milky Way escape velocity.

Gaia DR3 ADQL query used to select candidates

inclusion_assessment.summary: J0546+0836 is a newly discovered hot D^6 star in this paper. RV=1200+/-20 km/s, v_tot~1699 km/s, v_ejection~1864 km/s. Distance poorly constrained (4.02^+2.25_-1.67 kpc). The paper confirms all D^6 star velocities exceed the Milky Way escape velocity.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Core new discovery of the paper. Despite large distance uncertainty, the minimum ejection velocity far exceeds the Milky Way escape velocity.

distance.description: Bayesian kinematic distance. Large uncertainty due to poorly constrained parallax.

total_velocity.description: Total 3D space velocity. Large uncertainty from distance.

spectral_type.description: Oxygen-rich atmosphere with wind emission lines. Teff=95000K from Werner et al. (2024) NLTE analysis.

source_refs.context: One object with Teff above 100000K

candidate_groups_considered.description: D6-2 (Gaia DR3 1798008584396457088): known D^6 star from Shen 2018. Lowest velocity D^6: v_tot~1151 km/s, v_ejection~1051 km/s. Nearest D^6 at 0.84 kpc. Associated with supernova remnant G116.9+0.2 (flight time ~10^5 yr). Lowest-mass donor (0.15-0.5 M_sun). Still classified as Galactic-unbound per paper's statement that all D^6 stars will leave the Milky Way.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: D6-2 is a known D^6 star. Although its velocity is the lowest (ejection ~1051 km/s), the paper explicitly states all D^6 stars will leave the Milky Way. Already included in the candidate list. Listed separately here for mass/velocity binning reference.

source_refs.context: In the future, all of the D^6 stars will leave the Milky Way

candidate_groups_considered.description: J1332-3541 (Gaia DR3 6164642052589392512): new hot D^6 star. v_tot~1464 km/s, v_ejection~1619 km/s. Only D^6 with helium-dominated atmosphere. Lowest velocity among new discoveries. Paper classifies it as D^6 star, confirming unbound status.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: J1332-3541 is a newly discovered hot D^6 star in this paper. The only helium-dominated atmosphere. Although its velocity is lower among the D^6 stars, the paper still classifies it as a D^6 star and confirms it is unbound. Already included in the candidate list.

source_refs.context: Table tab:query: J1332 classified as hot D^6 star

candidate_groups_considered.description: LP 40-365 stars: partially burned runaway accretors. Includes LP 40-365 (known), J1603, J0905, J1825, J1311, J1109. Lower ejection velocities (~600-1300 km/s). Different abundance patterns and formation mechanism (possibly Type Iax SNe). Paper does not claim Galactic-unbound status.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: LP 40-365 stars have lower velocities (ejection velocity ~600-1300 km/s), and their formation mechanism may differ from D^6 stars (Iax supernovae vs D^6). The paper does not explicitly claim they are Galactic-unbound. Their velocities may be below the Milky Way escape velocity, outside the HVS candidate range.

source_refs.context: LP 40-365 stars: slower velocities ~600 km/s, different from D^6 scenario

candidate_groups_considered.description: US 708 (Gaia DR3 815106177700219392): known high-velocity helium star (sdO/B), v_tot~994 km/s, v_ejection~833 km/s. Not a WD. Previously known (Geier 2015).
candidate_groups_considered.reason: US 708 is a helium star (sdO/B), not a white dwarf. Ejection velocity ~833 km/s. The paper lists it separately but it does not belong to the D^6 stars. Not considered an HVS candidate from this paper.

source_refs.context: Table tab:all_systems: US 708 listed as runaway helium star donor

candidate_groups_considered.description: J1240 (oxygen-atmosphere WD) and J1637 (unknown classification): low and uncertain velocities. J1240: v_tot~239 km/s clearly bound. J1637: v_tot~1202 km/s but poorly constrained and not confirmed as thermonuclear runaway.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: J1240 has too low a velocity (~240 km/s) and is clearly bound to the Milky Way. J1637 has extremely poor distance constraints and unclear classification. The paper does not claim they are Galactic-unbound.

source_refs.context: Table tab:all_systems: Other/unknown section

candidate_groups_considered.description: Rejected candidates from primary ADQL query (Table tab:query): 12 sources classified as DA WD, sdO/B, or MS stars. All have |RV| < 500 km/s and normal hydrogen-line spectra. False positives from halo stars with underestimated parallaxes.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Spectroscopy confirmed these as false positives. They are halo stars (DA WD/sdO/B/MS) with normal spectra, where underestimated parallax caused artificially inflated apparent tangential velocities. All have RV < 500 km/s.

source_refs.context: False positives: halo stars with underestimated parallaxes

candidate_groups_considered.description: Secondary candidates (Table tab:query2): 21 lower-priority candidates. All have |RV| < 500 km/s, almost all show hydrogen lines. None are thermonuclear runaway candidates.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: All are normal-spectrum objects, |RV| < 500 km/s. The paper confirms none are good thermonuclear runaway candidates.

source_refs.context: None of these objects turned out to be good candidates
